methods.description: This method involves classifying lithological and textural features of geological deposits based on established frameworks for different types of materials, such as carbonates, epiclastic, and pyroclastic materials.

steps.description: Lithological and textural descriptions are based on Dunham (1962) for carbonates, Wentworth (1922) for epiclastic and Fisher and Schminke (1984) for pyroclastic material.
steps.searchable_summary: classify lithological and textural features using established frameworks

steps.description: Deposits are regarded as pyroclastic when they consist for >75% of primary pyroclastic material.
steps.searchable_summary: determine if deposits are pyroclastic based on material composition

steps.description: Paleosols have been described following the World Reference Base for soil resources of the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO, 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: describe paleosols using FAO World Reference Base

methods.description: This method recalculates GPS-measured heights to meters above sea level using a local geoid height.

steps.description: GPS-measured heights, referenced to the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS84) have been recalculated to meters above sea level (m + MSL) using a local geoid height of 25.142 m (source: Unavco).
steps.searchable_summary: recalculate GPS heights to meters above sea level using local geoid height
outputs: heights in meters above sea level

methods.description: Fieldwork conducted to study river terraces, including mapping, surveying, and analyzing terrace sediments and stratigraphy.

steps.description: Fieldwork was carried out in 2018 and 2019, studying riverside exposures and sand quarries between Sonde and Ngawi.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct fieldwork to study riverside exposures and sand quarries

steps.description: We prepared a 12-m resolution digital elevation model (DEM) based on TerraSAR-X/TanDEM-X satellite images to identify potential terrace surfaces.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare DEM from satellite images to identify terrace surfaces

steps.description: The surfaces were mapped and surveyed in the field and heights were measured by GPS and referenced with DEM-based heights.
steps.searchable_summary: map and survey terrace surfaces, measure heights with GPS

steps.description: Correlations between terraces were based on absolute and relative heights of terrace surface and (scour) base and facies of the terrace deposits.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate terraces using heights and facies data
